codeReview.summary: The feature implementation satisfies the m1 contract intent: mission creation defaults remain approval-gated, mission approval stamps actor metadata and transitions status as specified, and mission listing now includes a deterministic updatedAt-desc + missionID-asc comparator. I found one non-blocking test coverage gap.

issues.description: testVAL_DAEMON_003_MissionListTieBreakByMissionIDAscending does not force equal updatedAt values and only asserts that the most recently approved mission is first, so it can pass even if the missionID tie-break behavior regresses.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission contract-work documentation is stale relative to the implemented and validated deterministic tie-break semantics for mission listing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/albertonunez/.factory/missions/d1d1287f-f2a9-4a34-8ba9-fce37df13493/contract-work/daemon-authoring-planning.md:22 describes list ordering as only updatedAt descending, while /Users/albertonunez/.factory/missions/d1d1287f-f2a9-4a34-8ba9-fce37df13493/validation-contract.md:20 and MissionControlStore.swift enforce updatedAt DESC with missionID ASC tie-break.

summary: Reviewed worker session 5a2b357c-abe2-4a5a-98f1-761ff0cde5b5 and commit da48984689614b1bfd38653c797b4893b3886737 for m1-intent-ingestion-contract.
